entity.summary: Philippines-based cryptocurrency trading platform licensed by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas as a virtual currency exchange in September 2018 and currently listed as an active non-bank virtual asset service provider.
entity.notes: PDAX public materials use several milestones: founded in 2017, established and licensed in 2018, and officially launched in the Philippine market in 2019. HEI retains the existing 2018 year-level marker pending a stricter product-launch convention.

expected.summary: Philippines-based centralized exchange launched in 2018 and still operating under the PDAX brand.

changes.summary: Philippines-based cryptocurrency trading platform licensed by the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas as a virtual currency exchange in September 2018 and currently listed as an active non-bank virtual asset service provider.
changes.notes: PDAX public materials use several milestones: founded in 2017, established and licensed in 2018, and officially launched in the Philippine market in 2019. HEI retains the existing 2018 year-level marker pending a stricter product-launch convention.

events.title: PDAX received a Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas virtual-currency-exchange license
events.description: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas licensed PDAX to operate as a virtual currency exchange in September 2018.
events.notes: The reviewed official material establishes September 2018 but not a day, so the first day of the month is used as a month-level marker.

evidence.title: Is PDAX regulated?
evidence.notes: PDAX states that it received its BSP virtual-currency-exchange license in September 2018 under BSP Circular No. 944.

evidence.title: List of Virtual Asset Service Providers as of 31 May 2026
evidence.notes: The BSP directory lists Philippine Digital Asset Exchange (PDAX) among active non-bank VASPs.

evidence.title: PDAX official platform
evidence.notes: The official domain verifies the current PDAX identity and active trading platform.
